content.text: Academic Standards: Eduversal Partner Schools Network A Comprehensive Guide to Educational Leadership, Cambridge Standards, and Quality Management
content.title: Title

items: Current Version: 1.0
Effective Date: July 30, 2025
Next Review Date: January 2026
Review Cycle: Annual (with mid-year evaluation)
content.title: Version Control

items: Primary Owner: Yayasan Edukasi Universal Indonesia (Eduversal)
Custodian: Executive Director
Approval Authority: Eduversal Board of Trustees & Network General Managers
content.title: Document Ownership

content.text: This handbook is intended for:

items: Principals across all Eduversal Partner Schools
Senior Leadership Teams (SLT)
Eduversal Board of Trustees members
General Managers / Foundation Representatives
Quality Assurance Team
content.title: Distribution

steps: Proposed amendments submitted to the Executive Director
Review by the General Managers Committee (quarterly meetings)
Stakeholder consultation (schools, parents, staff, where applicable)
Board approval for substantive changes
Version update and re-distribution
Implementation timeline communicated network-wide
content.title: Amendment Process

items: Primary Language: English
EAL Considerations: Written in clear, accessible English suitable for non-native speakers
Indonesian Summaries: Key policies available in Bahasa Indonesia upon request
Digital Access: Available via the EduNav platform for authorized users
content.title: Language and Accessibility

content.text: Our Aspirational Future To be a transformative network of Cambridge schools across Indonesia, recognized internationally for academic excellence, innovative pedagogy, and developing globally competent, ethically grounded learners who thrive in a rapidly changing world.
content.title: VISION

content.text: Our Purpose and Commitments We exist to:

steps: Deliver world-class, Cambridge-standard education that challenges and supports every student to achieve their highest potential academically, socially, and emotionally.
Cultivate exceptional educators through continuous professional development, collaborative practice, and access to cutting-edge pedagogical approaches.
Prepare students for global competitiveness by providing opportunities in international academic competitions (including olympiads), university pathways, and real-world problem-solving.
Build a network of educational excellence by sharing best practices, resources, and expertise across our partner schools and the wider Indonesian education community.
Integrate Indonesian values and identity within an international curriculum framework, ensuring students are rooted in Pancasila principles while globally aware.
Provide comprehensive educational consultancy that elevates teaching quality, curriculum design, and institutional effectiveness across our network and beyond.
content.title: MISSION

content.text: The Principles That Guide Us
content.title: CORE VALUES

content.text: We set high standards and pursue continuous improvement in all aspects of our work. Every student and staff member can achieve excellence through effort, support, and reflective practice.
content.title: EXCELLENCE (Keunggulan)

content.text: We act with honesty, ethical responsibility, and transparency. Our decisions and actions align with our commitments to students, families, and the wider community.
content.title: INTEGRITY (Integritas)

content.text: We embrace change, take calculated risks, and continuously seek better ways to teach, learn, and lead. We leverage technology and research-informed practices to enhance learning outcomes.
content.title: INNOVATION (Inovasi)

content.text: We value diversity and ensure equitable access to high-quality education. We recognize and respond to the varied learning needs, backgrounds, and strengths of all students and staff.
content.title: INCLUSIVITY (Inklusivitas)

content.text: We believe in the power of teamwork, shared leadership, and learning communities. We collaborate within and across schools to achieve outcomes no single person or institution could accomplish alone.
content.title: COLLABORATION (Kolaborasi)

content.text: We develop students who understand their Indonesian identity while engaging confidently and responsibly with diverse cultures, perspectives, and global challenges. SOCIAL CONTRIBUTION (Pengabdian Sosial): We encourage students to contribute meaningfully to their communities, embodying empathy, responsibility, and global citizenship.
content.title: GLOBAL CITIZENSHIP (Kewarganegaraan Global)

content.text: The Capacities We Develop in Every Graduate By the time students complete their education within the Eduversal network, they will demonstrate:
content.title: SCHOOLWIDE LEARNER OUTCOMES (SLOs) (integrate with P8 Profile)?

items: Master rigorous content across disciplines aligned to Cambridge standards
Apply analytical reasoning, inquiry, and evidence-based problem solving
Demonstrate intellectual curiosity and self-directed learning habits
content.title: 1. Academic Excellence & Critical Thinking

items: Express ideas clearly and persuasively in English and Bahasa Indonesia, across written, oral, and digital formats
Listen actively and engage respectfully in dialogue with diverse audiences
Collaborate productively in teams and negotiate meaning across cultures
content.title: 2. Effective Communication

items: Understand Indonesian history, values (Pancasila), and cultural heritage
Engage knowledgeably with global issues, perspectives, and interdependencies
Navigate diverse cultural contexts with empathy, respect, and adaptability
content.title: 3. Global Competence & Cultural Awareness

items: Act with integrity, honesty, and respect for others
Take initiative to address challenges in their communities and beyond
Demonstrate environmental stewardship and commitment to sustainable practices
content.title: 4. Ethical Leadership & Social Responsibility

items: Apply creative thinking and design processes to solve complex problems
Use technology responsibly and effectively to enhance learning and productivity
Embrace change with resilience, flexibility, and a growth mindset
content.title: 5. Innovation & Adaptability

items: Practice self-regulation, reflection, and responsible decision-making
Maintain physical, mental, and emotional health through balanced living
Set and pursue meaningful goals with perseverance and self-discipline
content.title: 6. Personal Wellbeing & Self-Awareness

content.text: Eduversal Partner Schools operate under a collaborative governance model that balances:

items: Network coherence through shared standards, systems, and expertise
School autonomy in operational decision-making and contextual responsiveness
Accountability to students, families, regulatory bodies, and educational standards
content.title: Governance Philosophy

content.text: Yayasan Edukasi Universal Indonesia (Eduversal) Board of Trustees

items: Composition: 5-7 members with expertise in education, finance, law, and governance
Chair: Ketua Umum Yayasan
Term: 3-year terms, renewable
Meeting Frequency: Quarterly (minimum); additional meetings as needed
content.title: Level 1: Eduversal Foundation (Network Level)

content.text: Responsibilities:

items: Approve network-wide strategic direction and 5-year plans
Ensure financial sustainability and fiduciary oversight across the network
Appoint and evaluate the Chief Academic Officer and senior network leadership
Approve major capital expenditures, new school partnerships, and policy frameworks
Monitor compliance with Indonesian regulations, Cambridge standards, and accreditation requirements
Safeguard the mission, vision, and values of Eduversal

content.text: Executive Director

items: Reports to: Board of Trustees (via Chair)
Responsibilities:
Lead implementation of network strategy and academic standards
Oversee Quality Management System (QMS) across all schools
Manage staff rotation, appraisal, and professional development programs
Coordinate Cambridge, IB, Accreditation compliance, and examination integrity
Monitor school performance against KPIs and SLOs
Support General Managers with coaching and resources
Facilitate network-wide collaboration and knowledge sharing

content.text: Local Yayasan Role:

items: Legal owner of school facilities, land, and operational licenses
Holds NPSN and Ministry permits
Employer of record for all staff at the school
Financial sponsor for school operations per MOU with Eduversal
content.title: Level 2: Partner School Foundation (School Ownership Level)

content.text: Responsibilities:

items: Provide funding for operational expenses, salaries, and capital improvements
Ensure compliance with regulations and local licensing
Coordinate with the General Manager (Eduversal representative) on budget and reporting
Approve the annual school budget in consultation with Eduversal and GM
Participate in annual evaluation meetings with Eduversal leadership

content.text: Decision Rights:

items: Approve school-level annual budget (after draft by GM)
Approve major capital projects and facility changes
Review and endorse significant policy changes proposed by Eduversal/GM
Resolve disputes escalated by GM (in consultation with Eduversal)

content.text: Note: Day-to-day management authority is delegated to the General Manager per the 20-year partnership MOU.
General Manager (GM) / Foundation Representative (FR) Appointment: Assigned by Eduversal, serves as Eduversal's on-site representative with full management authority Reports to:

items: Functionally: Executive Director
Administratively: Local Yayasan (for budget execution and compliance reporting)
content.title: Level 3: School Leadership (Operational Level)

content.text: Responsibilities:

items: Full operational authority over school management, curriculum, staffing, and student affairs
Develop an annual School Improvement Plan (SIP) aligned to the network strategy
Draft and execute the annual school budget in coordination with the local Yayasan
Implement QMS programs (DTP, Appraisal, EduOS, EduNav, EASE)
Recruit, supervise, and evaluate principals, teachers, and staff.
Ensure safeguarding, child protection, and student well-being
Represent Eduversal in all dealings with local authorities, parents, and the community
Submit bi-annual reports to Eduversal and local Yayasan
Verify that all school-level data in Dapodik accurately reflects the school’s licensing, facility status, and NPSN registration
Ensure that the local Yayasan's funding and operational licenses are correctly aligned with the data submitted to the Ministry of Education
School administration should monitor official tax payments and other related official financial transactions.

content.text: Decision Rights:

items: Hire/terminate non-rational staff (in consultation with HR)
Approve curriculum adaptations within the Cambridge framework
Set the school calendar and daily operations
Manage student admissions and discipline (within policy)
Allocate budget within approved parameters
Emergency decisions (with post-hoc reporting)

content.text: Principal (Kepala Sekolah) Appointment: Assigned by the Foundation Representative / General Manager in consultation with Eduversal. Role Structure: Depending on school size and structure, the Principal's role may be organized by educational level:

steps: Early Years Principal: TK/KB (ages 3-6)
Primary School Principal: Grades 1-6 (Cambridge Primary)
Secondary School Principals:

items: Junior High School Principal: Grades 7-9 (Cambridge Lower Secondary, IGCSE)
Senior High School Principal: Grades 10-12 (IGCSE, AS/A Level)

content.text: In smaller schools, a single Principal may oversee multiple levels. In larger schools, separate Principals report to the Foundation Representative / General Manager and collaborate through the SLT. Core Competencies (Aligned with Permendiknas No. 13/2007): All Principals must demonstrate competency in:

steps: Kepribadian (Personality): Integrity, work ethic, self-development
Managerial: Planning, organizing, leading, monitoring
Kewirausahaan (Entrepreneurship): Innovation, risk-taking, school improvement
Supervision: Teacher observation, instructional coaching, quality assurance
Sosial: Collaboration, communication, community relations

content.text: Reports to: General Manager Responsibilities:

steps: Academic and instructional leadership at assigned level(s)
Implement the Cambridge curriculum and Indonesian national subjects
Lead Professional Learning Communities (PLCs) and teacher appraisal
Supervise Subject Leaders and department heads
Oversee student support services (EAL, SEN, Counseling)
Manage day-to-day student discipline and parent communication
Collaborate with Eduversal Directors (Primary/Secondary Education)
Support school improvement planning and evaluation
Ensure compliance with Cambridge Centre requirements.
Oversee the accuracy of teacher data within Dapodik and SIMPKB, ensuring all educators are properly registered under the correct NUPTK for appraisal and certification purposes.

Verify that student enrollment, attendance rules, and academic progression are recorded correctly in Dapodik to maintain valid student records for national examinations and the Profil Pelajar Pancasila (P8) documentation .
Coordinate with the Pengawas Sekolah (School Supervisor) to ensure that school-level data aligns with Ministry inspection protocols and the Panduan Kerja Kepala Sekolah.
Ensure that the integration of Indonesian mandatory subjects is accurately reflected in national reporting systems while maintaining Cambridge standards.

content.text: Decision Rights:

steps: Classroom observations and teacher coaching
Timetabling and class assignments within the assigned level
Student academic grouping and interventions
Co-curricular program design
Minor operational adjustments (within GM-approved policy)
Recommendation for teacher hiring/termination (final decision: GM)

content.text: Matrix Collaboration:

steps: Functional guidance: Eduversal Director of Primary/Secondary Education
Direct supervision: General Manager / Foundation Representative
Regulatory oversight: Pengawas Sekolah (School Supervisor)

content.text: Senior Leadership Team (SLT) Composition:

items: General Manager (Chair)
Principal
Vice Principal(s) / Wakil Kepala Sekolah (Academic, Student Affairs, Operations as applicable)
Heads of Department (HODs) for core subjects
EAL/SEN Coordinator
Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL)

content.text: Responsibilities:

items: Collaborative decision-making on school strategy and operations
Monitor progress on SIP goals and KPIs
Review data and drive school improvement
Ensure coherence across departments and programs
Model professional culture and leadership

content.text: The following roles support the Principal in delivering high-quality academic programs and student services:
content.title: School-Level Middle Leadership Roles

content.text: Reports to: Primary or Secondary Principal (depending on level) Collaborates with: Eduversal Head of Department (matrix support) Scope: School Subject Leaders are appointed for core curriculum areas:

steps: Cambridge Core Subjects: English, Mathematics, Science (integrated), Physics, Chemistry, Biology
Indonesian National Subjects: Bahasa Indonesia, Pendidikan Pancasila dan Kewarganegaraan (PPKn), Religion (Islamic Studies / other faiths)
Cross-Curricular Programs: STEAM integration
content.title: A. School Subject Leader (Koordinator Mata Pelajaran)

content.text: Key Responsibilities:

steps: Lead implementation of the Cambridge curriculum in the assigned subject
Develop subject-specific schemes of work, assessments, and resources
Mentor and coach subject teachers; conduct peer observations
Collaborate with network-level Heads of Departments on best practices
Support Olympiad preparation by providing subject expertise
Monitor student outcomes and recommend interventions
Ensure curriculum alignment with Cambridge standards and SLOs

content.text: Decision Rights:

steps: Recommend teaching resources and materials (within budget)
Design internal assessments and mark schemes
Organize subject-specific professional development
Provide input on teacher recruitment and development plans

content.text: Reports to: Primary / Secondary School Principal Collaborates with: Eduversal Head of Olympiad Coordinators (EduOS) Responsibilities:

steps: Identify and select students for academic competitions
Organize school-level olympiad training sessions
Coordinate participation in national/international competitions (mathematics, science, informatics)
Liaise with Subject Leaders for specialized coaching
Track student competition results and achievements
Promote Olympiad culture within the school
content.title: B. School Olympiad Coordinator

content.text: Decision Rights:

steps: Select students for competition participation
Schedule training sessions and mock competitions
Recommend external coaching resources (subject to GM approval)

content.text: It would be more appropriate to include the Junior School in the scope of responsibilities as well. Reports to: Secondary Principal Collaborates with: Eduversal Director of College Counselling Responsibilities:

steps: Guide students through university application processes (UCAS, Common App, local universities)
Support personal statement and portfolio development
Organize university fairs, campus visits, and alumni mentoring
Advise on scholarship opportunities and financial aid
Maintain university placement tracking and reporting
Coordinate with Subject Leaders on academic references
content.title: C. School College Counsellor (Secondary Schools Only)

content.text: Decision Rights:

steps: Recommend university application strategies
Organize career guidance events (subject to budget)
Provide references and recommendation letters

content.text: Reports to: Primary or Secondary Principal (depending on student age group) Collaborates with: Eduversal Director of Extracurricular Coordination Responsibilities:

steps: Plan and oversee co-curricular activities: sports, arts, clubs, and community service
Align activities with Profil Pelajar Pancasila and SLOs
Recruit and supervise activity coaches/instructors
Organize school events, competitions, and performances
Monitor student participation and engagement
Promote student leadership opportunities
content.title: D. School Extracurricular Coordinator

content.text: Decision Rights:

steps: Design co-curricular program offerings (subject to GM approval)
Schedule activities and events
Recommend external instructors or partnerships

content.text: To maintain Cambridge International School Centre status (see Section 3), schools must designate:
content.title: Cambridge Centre-Specific Roles

steps: Usually: Secondary Principal
Responsibility: Overall accountability for Cambridge Centre compliance, exam integrity, and quality assurance
Authority: Final decision-maker on Cambridge exam policies at the school level
content.title: A. Centre Head

steps: Reports to: Centre Head / Secondary Principal
Responsibility: Manage all aspects of Cambridge exam administration
Secure storage of exam materials
Candidate registration and entries
Invigilator training and exam conduct
Results processing and post-results services
Correspondence with Cambridge International
Authority: Operational control over exam logistics and security
Attend HoD meetings to align internal exams, Checkpoint, and IGCSE preparations with academic plans.
Collect feedback from HoDs to enhance exam quality and procedures.
Facilitate teachers’ participation in Cambridge certificate programs for professional growth and international qualification alignment.
content.title: B. Exams Officer

steps: Reports to: Centre Head / General Manager
Responsibility:
Internal quality assurance and moderation
Primary contact with Cambridge for standards and compliance
Annual Centre declarations and monitoring
Investigation of malpractice allegations
Authority: Recommend actions to ensure Cambridge standards compliance
content.title: C. Quality Nominee

content.text: For full details on Cambridge Centre governance, see Section 3: Cambridge Centre Compliance.

Purpose: To identify, disclose, and manage conflicts of interest that may compromise decision-making integrity. Scope: Applies to Board members, Eduversal leadership, General Managers, Principals, and SLT members. Definitions:

items: Conflict of Interest: A situation where personal, financial, or professional interests may influence or appear to influence professional judgment or decisions.
Apparent Conflict: Perception of conflict, even if none exists.

content.text: Disclosure Requirements:

items: Annual Declaration: All covered individuals complete a Conflict of Interest Declaration Form at the start of each fiscal year (Appendix 1-A).
Ongoing Duty: Immediate disclosure when a new conflict arises (e.g., family member applies for a job, business relationship with a vendor).

content.text: Examples of Conflicts:

items: Financial interest in a vendor or service provider to the school
Family member employed by or seeking employment with the network
Board/leadership position in a competing educational institution
Acceptance of gifts, hospitality, or benefits from stakeholders beyond nominal value

content.text: Management Procedures:

steps: Disclosure: Individual declares conflict in writing to Chair (Board) or Chief Academic Officer (Staff).
Review: Chair or CAO assesses materiality and recommends a management approach.
Management Options:

items: Recusal: Individual abstains from relevant discussions and decisions.
Divestment: Remove financial interest.
Monitoring: Increased transparency and oversight.
Prohibition: Activity or relationship must cease.

steps: Documentation: All conflicts and management actions are recorded in the Conflict of Interest Register.

Gifts and Hospitality Register: To prevent corruption, favoritism, or the appearance of bias, all gifts, favors, meals, or hospitality received in a professional capacity must be recorded in the Gifts and Hospitality Register (logbook or digital form). Note: this always happens when parents are visited. Primarily, those who stay far away assist with transportation and hotel reservations. Violations: Failure to disclose may result in disciplinary action, including removal from the position.

content.text: Annual Board Self-Evaluation Purpose: To assess Board effectiveness, identify areas for improvement, and enhance governance capacity. Process:

steps: Survey: Board members complete an anonymous self-evaluation survey (Appendix 1-B) covering:

items: Strategic oversight and direction
Financial stewardship
Policy development and compliance
Support for leadership
Board culture and dynamics
Individual contribution

steps: Analysis: Chair and Secretary compile results and identify themes.

Feedback from School Leadership: To enhance transparency and alignment, the Board also collects structured input from the General Manager and School Principals on how effectively the Board supports communication, decision-making, and strategic direction.
Reflection Session: The Board dedicates a meeting to discuss findings and develop an improvement plan.
Action Plan: Board commits to 2-3 priority improvement actions for the year (e.g., enhanced financial literacy training, more precise role boundaries, more robust stakeholder engagement).
Progress Review: Mid-year check-in on action plan implementation.

content.text: External Review: Every 3-5 years, the Board may commission an independent governance review to complement self-evaluation.
Purpose: To ensure systematic oversight, compliance, and strategic focus throughout the year.

In the organizational chart, where shall we put Guidance Counseling Coordinators? It does not align with CIS requirements. How is it possible to align with CIS or WASC? Link : https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qQhJuc\1RtQy-VTtx40Q3E25XnsTPNyt/edit To meet CIS Domain E (Student Support Services) and Domain G (Wellbeing), the "Guidance Counseling" role must be expanded beyond academic placement.

items: Student Wellbeing & Success Department:
University & Career Counselor: Focuses on Cambridge destinations (UCAS/Common App) and career pathways.
Social-Emotional Counselor: A mandatory requirement for CIS/WASC to support student mental health and restorative practices.
Placement: This department reports directly to the Principal but has a functional reporting line to the General Manager for safeguarding and resource allocation.

content.text: Network-Wide Organizational Structure \- Eduversal Partner Schools BOARD OF TRUSTEES │ (Yayasan Edukasi Universal Indonesia) CHIEF ACADEMIC OFFICER SCHOOL 1 │ │ SCHOOL 2 │ │ SCHOOL 3 │ │ SCHOOL 4 │ SCHOOL-LEVEL STRUCTURE Local Yayasan (e.g., Y-KBM) ├─ Legal Owner ├─ Funding Provider └─ Compliance Oversight General Manager / Foundation Representative ├─ Full Operational Authority ├─ Appointed by Eduversal └─ Reports to: Chief Academic Officer (functional) \+ Yayasan (administrative) Add Early Years Principal (Kepala Sekolah PAUD) Primary Principal (Kepala Sekolah SD) ├─ Reports to: GM ├─ Collaborates with: Director of Primary Education (Eduversal) └─ Supervises: ├─ Primary Subject Leaders (English, Math, Science, Religion, PPKn, Bahasa Indonesia, STEAM) │ └─ Primary Extracurricular Coordinator │ └─ Secondary Principal (Kepala Sekolah SMP/SMA) ├─ Reports to: GM ├─ Collaborates with: Director of Secondary Education (Eduversal) └─ Supervises: ├─ Secondary Subject Leaders (Math, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Religion, STEAM, …) ├─ School Olympiad Coordinator ├─ School College Counsellor ├─ School Extracurricular Coordinator ├─ Cambridge Exams Officer └─ Quality Nominee (may overlap with GM or Principal) Senior Leadership Team (SLT) ├─ General Manager (Chair) ├─ Primary Principal ├─ Secondary Principal ├─ Vice Principal(s) (if applicable) ├─ Selected Subject Leaders (rotating representation) ├─ EAL/SEN Coordinator ├─ Designated Safeguarding Lead (DSL) └─ Cambridge Quality Nominee Teaching & Support Staff ├─ Rotational Staff (assigned by Eduversal: 3-year contracts) └─ Non-Rotational Staff (hired locally by GM) To ensure regulatory safety in Indonesia, specific oversight of Dapodik and P8 is required within the leadership roles.

items: The Registrar / Data Compliance Officer:
Role: Manages Dapodik, NPSN, and SIMPKB workflows.
Reporting Line: Reports to the General Manager to ensure that local Yayasan funding and legal status (licenses) are synchronized with national data.
P8 Coordinator (Projek Penguatan Profil Pelajar Pancasila):
Role: Ensures the integration of Indonesian values into the international framework.
Reporting Line: Reports to the Principal and collaborates with Subject Leaders to document the mandatory P8 projects.

content.text: NETWORK SUPPORT FUNCTIONS (Centralized by Eduversal):

items: Quality Management System (QMS) Team
HR & Staff Development
Cambridge Examinations Coordination
Accreditation & Compliance Support
IT & EduNav Platform Management
Financial Planning & Audit Support

content.text: To demonstrate effective governance and support accreditation, the following documents and records must be maintained: Board-Level Evidence:

items: Board Terms of Reference (Bylaws) and the 20-year Partnership MOUs (or interim Service Level Agreements for schools in transition)
Board meeting agendas, minutes, and resolutions (past 3 years)
Annual governance calendar
Board self-evaluation surveys and action plans
Annual Conflict of Interest Register and the Gifts and Hospitality Log
Strategic Plan (5-year) with annual updates
Network aggregate budget and financial reports
Chief Academic Officer performance evaluation
Board Self-Evaluation survey results and subsequent Governance Improvement Action Plans

content.text: School-Level Evidence:

items: Partnership MOUs (20-year agreements with local Yayasan) This is not available in some schools, especially Kesatuan Bangsa. We have talked several times at the HQ since the 2023-2024 academic year.
Organizational charts (network and school-specific)
General Manager job description and performance contract
School Improvement Plans (SIPs) with KPI dashboards
Bi-annual reports to Eduversal and local Yayasan
Stakeholder meeting minutes (GM-Yayasan coordination)
Decision logs demonstrating the use of the decision matrix
Valid National School Identification Number (NPSN) documentation and current school operational licenses
Teacher registration records in SIMPKB showing active NUPTK status for certified educators.
CIS/WASC Evidence: The "Standards Crosswalk" mapping school activities to accreditation domains.
Student Wellbeing: Records of socio-emotional counseling sessions and university placement data.
Exam Integrity: Cambridge secure storage audit logs, invigilator training certificates, and incident logs.
content.title: Accreditation & Quality Assurance Evidence

content.text: This section aligns with: International Standards:

steps: CIS Standards: Purpose & Direction; Governance, Leadership & Ownership
WASC Category A: Organization for Student Learning (A1-A5)
Cambridge Centre Requirements: Governance structures that ensure exam integrity and quality assurance

content.text: Indonesian Regulations:

steps: UU No. 20/2003 (Sisdiknas): National Education System Law
Permendiknas No. 13/2007: Standards for School Principals (Competency Framework)
Permendikbud No. 6/2018: Assignment of Teachers as School Principals
Civil Code (KUHPerdata) on Yayasan Governance: Yayasan (Foundation) governance requirements
MoE Oversight: Pengawas Sekolah (School Supervisor) supervision protocols
Panduan Kerja Kepala Sekolah (2017): Official Handbook for School Principals (JUKNIS)
Kurikulum Merdeka (P8): Requirements for the Projek Penguatan Profil Pelajar Pancasila to ensure local values are embedded in the curriculum

content.text: Reference Documents:

steps: JUKNIS: Panduan Kerja Kepala Sekolah, Direktorat Jenderal Guru dan Tenaga Kependidikan (2017)
Available in Section 20: References and Compliance Library
